notes: Software multiple claim: well-bound to Hormozi, specific conditions (viral growth, gross margin, churn), clear mechanism. Falsifiable via acquirer behavior.
Motivation source: named speaker, conditional framing, load-bearing (changes judgment), falsifiable via outcome measurement.
AI displacement: bounded to B2B, real-time demonstration, named entities, falsifiable via AI advancement and creator survival data.
Behavioral reinforcement: mechanism-based (operant conditioning), load-bearing (diagnosis method), falsifiable via testing.
Uncertainty tolerance: named speaker, clear conditions, mechanism (double uncertainty aversion), falsifiable via decision pattern analysis. Well-bounded.

failure_modes: A5-adjacent: Conditions array is excessively redundant (10 items with 3+ near-duplicates: 'B2C or B2B education', 'education or info product', 'pure education product with no ongoing consumable component' stated separately). Suggests over-cautious extraction. Should consolidate to 2-3 distinct conditions.
notes: Education ceiling claim is strong on all other axes (load-bearing, mechanism clear, falsifiable), but conditions list obscures referent clarity. Row 1 fails due to redundant/bloated conditions array rather than actual actor binding failure.
Activity-based time valuation: quantified with dual anchors ($500k/month, $50k delta = 2.5 days), named actor (founder), falsifiable via opportunity cost arithmetic.
Founder performance decay: quantified (30% decay), axis-anchored (100% baseline vs 70%), mechanism stated, falsifiable via delegation outcomes.
Value bifurcation: mechanism-based (post-transformation churn), conditions specified (education/coaching), load-bearing (pricing strategy changes), falsifiable.
Psychological barriers: hedonic adaptation claim, named speaker, mechanism (genetic set-point), load-bearing (happiness model), falsifiable via cohort testing.
Time-cost positioning: named entities ($70k threshold), comparison framing, mechanism (time scarcity > money scarcity for high-income), bounded (excludes low-income), falsifiable.
Content-offer congruence: quantified outcomes (+26% opt-ins, 2x book sales), time-anchored (3-4 weeks), named books ($100M Offers), axis-grounded, falsifiable via audience-content alignment.
Profitability over VC speed: named actor (Hormozi), specific context (TikTok Shop, $3.6M Y1), mechanism (cash availability → skill vs spending), falsifiable via founder behavior.
